data.title: Metropolitan Board Adopts Two-Year Budget, Commits to Addressing Aging Infrastructure, Future Reliability
data.description: In Los Angeles, the Metropolitan Water District’s Board of Directors has approved a two-year budget aimed at maintaining infrastructure and progressing a new recycled water project. The budget totals $2.3 billion for 2026/27 and $2.4 billion for 2027/28, with a 6.2% overall rate increase effective from Jan. 1. This decision was made unanimously to ensure the continued provision of safe water to communities in Southern California.

data.title: CCTP by Circle enables pay-first settlement for USDC cross-chain payouts, letting platforms delegate payments and settle reimbursements later.
data.description: Circle recently introduced a USDC payment system that operates via fulfillers, enabling platforms to delegate payouts. This new architecture utilizes CCTP and allows for reimbursements to be settled cross-chain at a later time. The innovative approach streamlines processes by assigning payment responsibilities to fulfillers, enhancing efficiency within the system.

data.title: WuXi Biologics Achieves ISO 13485 Certification, Signifying Strong Capabilities in Drug-Device Combination Product Development and Manufacturing
data.description: A Chinese company has become one of the pioneers in China's drug-device combination products industry to receive certification, showcasing its strong commitment to providing top-notch solutions to clients worldwide. This achievement is highlighted by the fact that the company had zero non-conformities, underscoring the effectiveness of its quality management system. The development was announced on April 14, 2026, in Shanghai.

data.title: Spyre Therapeutics Announces Pricing of Upsized $403.0 Million Public Offering of Common Stock
data.description: WALTHAM, Massachusetts, April 14, 2026— Today, Spyre Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: SYRE), a biotechnology company specializing in antibody engineering for immune-mediated diseases like Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD), revealed the pricing of its recent public offering. The offering consists of 6,500,000 shares of common stock set at $62.00 per share for a total of approximately $403.0 million in gross proceeds for the Company. This amount excludes underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses. As part of this offering, the underwriters have the option to purchase an additional roughly $60.5 million in common stock over the next 30 days at the same public offering price, with deductions for the underwriting discount.

data.title: OpenAI updates macOS certificates post Axios incident. Users urged to update ChatGPT, Codex apps by May 8 after npm compromise.
data.description: In light of the recent Axios npm compromise linked to North Korea, OpenAI is taking action by rotating their code signing certificates. This means that macOS users will need to update their ChatGPT and Codex apps by May 8th.

data.title: Surprise visit from prosecutors to Fed amid criminal inquiry may cause delay in confirming new chair.
data.description: A criminal investigation by the Justice Department into the Federal Reserve is causing potential disruption in confirming the next chairperson. This inquiry may lead to a delay in the appointment process, raising concerns about the transition of leadership within the organization. The exact impact of the investigation on the confirmation timeline remains uncertain, but it has created uncertainty and could potentially affect the smooth succession of leadership.
